The [[Mueller Report]], a summary of the findings of the [[Mueller special counsel investigation|Special Counsel investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 U.S. elections]], contained passing references to some of the dossier's allegations but little mention of its more sensational claims. It was a major subject of the [[Nunes memo]], the Democratic rebuttal memo,<ref name="Jalonick_Day_2/25/2018">{{cite news |last1=Jalonick |first1=Mary |last2=Day |first2=Chad |title=Congress releases redacted, declassified Democratic memo |url=https://apnews.com/d4d838725aa34441b5ae018c31e609a4 |access-date=August 1, 2020 |agency=[[Associated Press]] |date=February 25, 2018}}</ref> and the [[Inspector General report on the Crossfire Hurricane investigation]].
The investigations led to Fusion GPS founder Glenn Simpson being interviewed in August 2017 by Congress.<ref name="Lucas_8/23/2017">{{cite news |last=Lucas |first=Ryan |title=Researcher Behind Unverified Trump Dossier Meets Senate Investigators |work=[[NPR]] |date=August 23, 2017 |url=https://www.npr.org/2017/08/23/545289362/researcher-behind-unverified-trump-dossier-meets-senate-investigators |access-date=August 12, 2021}}</ref>
[[Durham special counsel investigation|John Durham has been investigating]] whether FBI agents "mishandled classified information" about operation Crossfire Hurricane when they questioned Steele. The IG report documents that a case agent mentioned Papadopoulos to Steele. The FBI has no "established guidelines for how to address the disclosure of sensitive or classified information to sources", and the Inspector General "concluded that the case agent should not be faulted".<ref name="Bertrand_12/12/2020" />
Durham sought to get more information by seeking access to evidence gathered in a [[#Against Steele and Orbis Business Intelligence. Filed by Fridman, Aven, and Khan|British lawsuit filed by the founders of Alfa-Bank]]. Durham's efforts were only partially successful. A July 21, 2020, court filing shows that Durham has sought the lifting of "a protective order on evidence that had been gathered". ''Politico'' wrote that legal experts said this move was "highly unusual ... and suggests the British government was not involved in, or cooperating with, Durham's criminal investigation".<ref name="Bertrand_12/12/2020">{{cite web |last=Bertrand |first=Natasha |author-link=Natasha Bertrand |date=December 12, 2020 |title=Durham sought records belonging to British ex-spy Chris Steele |newspaper=[[Politico]] |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2020/12/12/durham-british-spy-records-444665 |access-date=December 13, 2020}}</ref>
== Conspiracy theories and claims about dossier == === Conspiracy theory it was trigger for start of investigation ===
The [[Russia investigation origins conspiracy theory]]<ref name="Kilgore4/4/2017">{{cite magazine |last=Kilgore |first=Ed |title=Susan Rice Becomes the Face of the Trump Counter-Narrative on Russia |magazine=[[New York (magazine)|New York]] |date=April 4, 2017 |url=https://nymag.com/intelligencer/2017/04/susan-rice-becomes-face-of-trump-counter-narrative-on-russia.html |access-date=November 11, 2019}}</ref> is a disproven right-wing [[Alternative facts|alternative narrative]],<ref name="Smerconish_10/12/2019">{{cite news |last=Smerconish |first=Michael |author-link=Michael Smerconish |title=Why is Barr investigating origins of Russia Probe? |work=[[CNN]] |date=October 12, 2019 |url=https://www.cnn.com/videos/tv/2019/10/12/why-is-barr-investigating-origins-of-russia-probe.cnn |access-date=November 11, 2019}}</ref><ref name="Walsh_7/15/2018">{{cite magazine |last=Walsh |first=David A. |title=How the right-wing convinces itself that liberals are evil |magazine=[[Washington Monthly]] |date=July 15, 2018 |url=https://washingtonmonthly.com/magazine/july-august-2018/how-the-right-wing-convinces-itself-that-liberals-are-evil/ |access-date=November 11, 2019}}</ref> sometimes identified as a set of conspiracy theories,<ref name="Wittes_5/17/2023" /><ref name="Lutz_7/24/2019" /><ref name="Timm_9/25/2019">{{cite news |last=Timm |first=Jane C. |title=Trump promotes conspiracy theory: Clinton's deleted emails are in Ukraine |work=[[NBC News]] |date=September 25, 2019 |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/trump-impeachment-inquiry/trump-promotes-conspiracy-theory-clinton-s-deleted-emails-are-ukraine-n1058726 |access-date=November 11, 2019}}</ref><ref>Multiple sources: * {{cite news |last=Sullivan |first=Andy |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-usa-trump-whistleblower-barr-explaine-idUSKBN1WG4QZ |title=Explainer: Barr gives top priority to investigating the investigators of Russian meddling |date=October 1, 2019 |work=[[Reuters]] |access-date=October 21, 2019 |ref=none}} * {{cite news |last1=Zapotosky |first1=Matt |last2=Dawsey |first2=Josh |author-link2=Josh Dawsey |last3=Harris |first3=Shane |author-link=Shane Harris |last4=Helderman |first4=Rosalind |author-link4=Rosalind S. Helderman |title=Barr's review of Russia investigation wins Trump's favor. Those facing scrutiny suspect he's chasing conspiracy theories. |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=October 6, 2019 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/barrs-review-of-the-russia-investigation-has-won-trumps-favor-but-some-facing-scrutiny-suspect-hes-chasing-conspiracy-theories/2019/10/05/cea0f994-e612-11e9-a331-2df12d56a80b_story.html |access-date=November 11, 2019 |ref=none}} * {{cite web |last=Ward |first=Alex |title=The right-wing conspiracy theories behind Trump and Barr's outreach to foreign leaders | website=[[Vox (website)|Vox]] |date=September 30, 2019 |url=https://www.vox.com/world/2019/9/30/20892262/trump-barr-ukraine-australia-italy-uk-conspiracy |access-date=November 11, 2019 |ref=none}}</ref> concerning the origins of the original [[Crossfire Hurricane (FBI investigation)|Crossfire Hurricane investigation]] and the following [[Mueller special counsel investigation|Special Counsel investigation]] into [[Russian interference in the 2016 United States elections]]. The theory asserts that Trump "was targeted by politically biased Obama officials to prevent his election",<ref name="Waas_11/2/2020" /> and that the Steele dossier triggered the Russia probe.<ref name="Kruzel_7/23/2018" /> These conspiracy theories<ref name="Blake_1/2/2018">{{cite news |last=Blake |first=Aaron |title=Republicans' Steele dossier conspiracy theory was dealt a big blow this weekend |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=January 2, 2018 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/the-fix/wp/2018/01/02/republicans-steele-dossier-conspiracy-theory-was-dealt-a-big-blow-this-weekend/ |access-date=November 6, 2019}}</ref><ref name="Shear_Benner_Fandos_8/17/2018">{{cite news |first1=Michael D. |last1=Shear |author-link1=Michael D. Shear |first2=Katie |last2=Benner |author-link2=Katie Benner |first3=Nicholas |last3=Fandos |author-link3=Nicholas Fandos |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2018/08/17/us/politics/trump-conspiracy-bruce-nellie-ohr.html |title=Embracing Conspiracy Theory, Trump Escalates Attack on Bruce Ohr |date=August 17, 2018 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]]}}</ref> have been pushed by Trump,<ref name="Kruzel_7/23/2018">{{cite web |last=Kruzel |first=John |title=Trump falsely says Steele dossier triggered Russia probe | website=[[PolitiFact]] |date=July 23, 2018 |url=https://www.politifact.com/truth-o-meter/statements/2018/jul/23/donald-trump/donald-trump-falsely-says-new-carter-page-document/ |access-date=April 12, 2019}}</ref> Fox News,<ref name="Rupar_03/22/2019">{{cite web |last=Rupar |first=Aaron |title=Fox News has normalized a lie about the origins of the Russia investigation | website=[[Vox (website)|Vox]] |date=March 22, 2019 |url=https://www.vox.com/2019/3/22/18277089/fox-news-steele-dossier-lie-trump-witch-hunt |access-date=March 23, 2019}}</ref> Republican politicians like Representative [[Jim Jordan]] (R-Ohio),<ref name="Blake_1/2/2018" /> Trump's [[Director of National Intelligence]] [[John Ratcliffe (American politician)|John Ratcliffe]],<ref name="Robertson_3/27/2019">{{cite web |last=Robertson |first=Lori |title=Dossier Not What 'Started All of This' | website=[[FactCheck.org]] |date=March 27, 2019 |url=https://www.factcheck.org/2019/03/dossier-not-what-started-all-of-this/ |access-date=August 30, 2020}}</ref> and Trump's Attorney General [[William Barr]].<ref name="Waas_11/2/2020" />
In spite of these allegations, on November 2, 2020, the day before the presidential election, ''New York'' magazine reported that:<ref name="Waas_11/2/2020">{{cite magazine |last=Waas |first=Murray |author-link=Murray Waas |title=How Trump and Barr's October Surprise Went Bust |magazine=[[New York (magazine)|New York]] |date=November 2, 2020 |url=https://nymag.com/intelligencer/2020/11/durham-investigation-how-trumps-october-surprise-went-bust.html |access-date=November 4, 2020}}</ref> {{blockquote| there has been no evidence found, after 18 months of investigation, to support Barr's claims that Trump was targeted by politically biased Obama officials to prevent his election. (The probe remains ongoing.) In fact, the sources said, the Durham investigation has so far uncovered no evidence of any wrongdoing by Biden or Barack Obama, or that they were even involved with the Russia investigation. There 'was no evidence ... not even remotely ... indicating Obama or Biden did anything wrong,' as one person put it.}}
The conspiracy theory falsely claims the dossier triggered the [[Crossfire Hurricane (FBI investigation)|Russia investigation]] and was used as an excuse by the FBI to start it. It also aims to discredit Steele and thus discredit the whole investigation.<ref name="Lamond_Dessel_10/17/2019">{{cite web |last1=Lamond |first1=James |last2=Dessel |first2=Talia |title=Bill Barr's Witch Hunt |publisher=[[New York University School of Law|Just Security]] |date=October 17, 2019 |url=https://www.justsecurity.org/66609/bill-barrs-witch-hunt/ |access-date=November 12, 2019}}</ref> The real trigger for the July 31 opening of the investigation was two connected events: the July 22 release by [[WikiLeaks]] of Democratic National Committee emails stolen by Russian hackers and the July 26 revelation by the Australian government of the bragging by Papadopoulos of Russian offers to aid the Trump campaign by releasing those emails.<ref name="Savage_12/1/2021" /><ref name="NCA_11/18/2018">{{Cite news |date=November 18, 2018 |title=Former Donald Trump aide George Papadopoulos, who was outed by Alexander Downer asks for prison delay |url=https://www.news.com.au/finance/work/leaders/former-donald-trump-aide-george-papadopoulos-who-was-outed-by-alexander-downer-asks-for-prison-delay/news-story/cd7cb4c76fce62b94aae1b2f3fbe6aac |work=[[News.com.au]] |publisher=[[News Corp Australia]] |access-date=August 22, 2022 |quote=This information has been described as the starting point that led to an FBI investigation and then the Mueller probe into Russia's efforts to interfere with the 2016 election.}}</ref>
The dossier could not have had any role in the opening of the Russia investigation on July 31, 2016, as top FBI officials received the dossier much later on September 19.<ref name="Goldman_Savage_4/9/2019">{{cite news |last1=Goldman |first1=Adam |last2=Savage |first2=Charlie |author-link2=Charlie Savage (author) |title=Justice Dept. Watchdog's Review of Russia Inquiry Is Nearly Done, Barr Says |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=April 9, 2019 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2019/04/09/us/politics/russia-investigation-barr.html |access-date=April 12, 2019}}</ref><ref name="Bump_5/17/2023">{{cite news | last=Bump | first=Philip | title=There's now even less reason to blame Clinton for the Russia probe | newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] | date=May 17, 2023 | url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/05/17/clinton-durham-report/ | access-date=August 29, 2023 | quote=That the dossier didn't make it to the team that was investigating interference until this late point means it was impossible that the predicate for the investigation was those reports. What's more, there was clear reason to launch the probe: specifically, the information from the Australians and the various points at which Trump's team was intertwined with Russian interests.}}</ref> Instead, it was the activities of George Papadopoulos that started the investigation.<ref name="Brannen_8/20/2019">{{cite web |last=Brannen |first=Kate |title=Expert Summaries of Mueller Report: A Collection |publisher=[[New York University School of Law|Just Security]] |date=August 20, 2019 |url=https://www.justsecurity.org/65863/expert-summaries-of-mueller-report-a-collection-of-short-essays/ |access-date=November 12, 2019}}</ref> The investigation by Inspector General Michael E. Horowitz into Russian interference and alleged FISA abuses found that "none of the evidence used to open the [original Crossfire Hurricane FBI] investigation" came from the C.I.A. or Trump–Russia dossier.<ref name="Goldman_Savage_11/22/2019">{{cite news |last1=Goldman |first1=Adam |last2=Savage |first2=Charlie |author-link2=Charlie Savage (author) |title=Russia Inquiry Review Is Said to Criticize F.B.I. but Rebuff Claims of Biased Acts |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=November 22, 2019 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2019/11/22/us/politics/russia-investigation-inspector-general-report.html |access-date=November 23, 2019}}</ref> On February 4, 2018, Rep. [[Trey Gowdy]] (R-S.C.) affirmed that the Russia probe would have happened without the dossier: "So there's going to be a Russia probe, even without a dossier."<ref name="Robertson_2/7/2018" /><ref name="Robertson_3/27/2019" /> Also in February 2018, the [[Nunes memo]] stated: "The Papadopoulos information triggered the opening of an FBI counterintelligence investigation in late July 2016 by FBI agent [[Peter Strzok|Pete Strzok]]."<ref name="Bush_Alcindor_Wellford_Sreenivasan_2/2/2018">{{Cite news |last1=Bush |first1=Daniel |last2=Alcindor |first2=Yamiche |author-link2=Yamiche Alcindor |last3=Wellford |first3=Rachel |last4=Sreenivasan |first4=Hari |author-link4=Hari Sreenivasan |title=We annotated the full Nunes memo on the Russia probe |date=February 2, 2018 |url=https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/we-annotated-the-full-nunes-memo-on-the-russia-probe |work=[[PBS NewsHour]] |access-date=August 19, 2022}}</ref> FBI Deputy Director [[Andrew McCabe]] mentioned both the investigation and the FISA warrants:<ref name="Robertson_3/27/2019" /> {{blockquote| 'We started the investigations without the dossier. We were proceeding with the investigations before we ever received that information,' McCabe told CNN. 'Was the dossier material important to the [FISA] package? Of course, it was. As was every fact included in that package. Was it the majority of what was in the package? Absolutely not.'}}
[[Benjamin Wittes]] has noted how the [[Durham special counsel investigation]] was launched because of such conspiracy theory allegations:<ref name="Wittes_5/17/2023" /> {{blockquote| It dealt, bizarrely, with the question of whether the FBI was lying about the origins of the Russia investigation. The FBI had claimed—and Special Counsel Robert Mueller had affirmed—that the whole thing started when an Australian diplomat named Alexander Downer provided the U.S. with information that a Trump campaign advisor named George Papadopoulos had volunteered in a London meeting over drinks that the Russians had 'dirt' on Clinton in the form of 'thousands of emails.' But a bunch of Trump supporters ginned up a set of conspiracy theories that this was not how the investigation started, that it all started with Steele, or some secret informant, or that the CIA was involved somehow.}}
The [[Durham Report]] affirmed that "the information [about Papadopoulos] . . . was the sole basis cited by the FBI for opening a full investigation into individuals associated with the ongoing Trump campaign".<ref name="Wittes_5/17/2023">{{cite web | last=Wittes | first=Benjamin | title=Notes on the Durham Report: A Reading Diary | website=[[Lawfare (website)|Lawfare]] | date=May 17, 2023 | url=https://www.lawfaremedia.org/notes-durham-report-reading-diary | access-date=June 23, 2023}}</ref> Durham also confirmed "it was not until mid-September that the Crossfire Hurricane investigators received several of the Steele Reports".<ref name="Cohen_5/15/2023">{{cite web | last=Cohen | first=Zachary | title=Takeaways from special counsel John Durham's report on FBI's Russia-Trump probe |work=CNN Politics | date=May 15, 2023 | url=https://www.cnn.com/2023/05/15/politics/durham-report-takeaways/index.html | access-date=June 23, 2023}}</ref><ref name="Cohen_10/11/2022" /> {{blockquote| Durham "debunked a prolific Trump lie about the Steele dossier – that it was the reason why the FBI started investigating his campaign in 2016. ... This false claim has been refuted dozens of times over the years, in official Justice Department documentation, bipartisan reports from Congress, and numerous court filings. ... The situation was all the more interesting because Trump has repeatedly acted as a cheerleader for Durham. ... On Tuesday, Durham inadvertently affirmed a basic truth about the Russia probe that Trump has lied about for years.}}
In April 2018, the Associated Press reported that:<ref name="Dems_memo_2/24/2018" /> {{blockquote| Christopher Steele's raw intelligence reporting did not inform the FBI's decision to initiate its counterintelligence investigation in late July 2016. In fact, the FBI's closely-held investigative team only received Steele's reporting in mid-September - more than seven weeks later. The FBI - and, subsequently, the Special Counsel's - investigation into links between the Russian government and Trump campaign associates has been based on troubling law enforcement and intelligence information unrelated to the 'dossier.'}}
[[Heather Digby Parton]] described why we should "forget the Steele dossier" as a "reason for the Russia investigation":<ref name="Parton_12/1/2021">{{cite web | last=Parton | first=Heather Digby |author-link=Digby (blogger) | title=Forget the Steele dossier: Mueller report release shows why Trump-Russia inquiry was required | website=[[Salon.com|Salon]] | date=December 1, 2021 | url=https://www.salon.com/2021/12/01/forget-the-steele-dossier-mueller-report-release-shows-why-inquiry-was-required/ | access-date=December 2, 2021}}</ref> {{blockquote| No doubt there was some histrionic coverage of the Steele Dossier. But the truth is that virtually every news outlet that reported it made clear that it was unsubstantiated and no one reported that it was the only reason for the Russia investigation. Trump and his campaign's suspicious behavior was more than enough to set off alarms all over the world.}}
=== Claim it was "a significant portion" of FISA application ===
On April 18, 2017, CNN reported that, according to U.S. officials, information from the dossier had been used as part of the basis for getting the October 2016 [[FISA]] warrant to monitor Page.<ref name="Perez_Prokupecz_Raju_4/18/2017" /><ref name="Nakashima_Barrett_Entous_4/11/2017" /> The Justice Department's inspector general revealed in 2019 that in the six weeks prior to its receipt of Steele's memos, the FBI's Crossfire Hurricane team "had discussions about the possibility of obtaining FISAs targeting Page and Papadopoulos, but it was determined that there was insufficient information at the time to proceed with an application to the court."<ref name="OIG_12/9/2019" />{{rp|101}}
The role of evidence from the dossier in seeking FISA warrants soon became the subject of much debate. How much of the evidence was based on the dossier? Was it a "significant portion"<ref name="Brown_Jarrett_3/17/2018" /> or only a "smart part" of the FISA application?<ref name="Dilanian_7/23/2018" />
In February 2018, the [[Nunes memo]] alleged FBI Deputy Director [[Andrew McCabe]]'s testimony backed Republican claims that the "dossier formed 'a significant portion' of the Carter Page FISA application".<ref name="Brown_Jarrett_3/17/2018" /> McCabe pushed back and said his testimony had been "selectively quoted" and "mischaracterized".<ref name="Brown_Jarrett_3/17/2018">{{cite web | last1=Brown | first1=Pamela | last2=Jarrett | first2=Laura | title=McCabe says Republicans 'mischaracterized' his testimony on Trump dossier | website=[[CNN]] | date=March 17, 2018 | url=https://www.cnn.com/2018/03/16/politics/mccabe-republicans-trump-dossier/index.html | access-date=June 19, 2023}}</ref> He also "denied having ever told Congress that the [FISA] warrant would not have been sought without information from the dossier".<ref name="McNamara_11/10/2020 a906">{{cite web | last=McNamara | first=Audrey | title=Andrew McCabe defends Trump campaign Russia probe during partisan hearing | website=[[CBS News]] | date=November 10, 2020 | url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/andrew-mccabe-testifies-russia-probe-watch-live-stream-today-2020-11-10/ | access-date=June 19, 2023}}</ref>
[[Adam Schiff]] said the FBI had a "wholly independent basis for investigating Page's long-established connections to Russia, aside from the Steele dossier".<ref name="Grant_Rosenberg_12/14/2018_2" /> Before the Crossfire Hurricane team received dossier material on September 19, 2016,{{efn|name="9/19/2016"}} they had already gathered enough evidence from their own sources to make them seriously consider seeking FISA warrants on Carter Page, but they needed a bit more, and, because "the Justice Department possessed information 'obtained through multiple independent sources that corroborated Steele's reporting' with respect to Page",<ref name="Grant_Rosenberg_12/14/2018_2" /> the mutually independent corroborations gave them more confidence to make that decision.
The IG report described a changed situation after the FBI received Steele's memos and said the dossier then played a "central and essential role" in the seeking of FISA warrants on [[Carter Page]]<ref name="OIG_12/9/2019" /> in terms of establishing FISA's low bar<ref name="Sanchez_12/11/2019" /> for [[probable cause]]: "FBI and Department officials told us the Steele reporting 'pushed [the FISA proposal] over the line' in terms of establishing probable cause."<ref name="OIG_12/9/2019" />{{rp|412}}<ref name="Herridge_Hymes_Segers_Quinn_12/9/2019" />
FBI Deputy Director [[Andrew McCabe]] mentioned the dossier's role in the start of the investigation and the FISA warrant:<ref name="Robertson_3/27/2019" /> {{blockquote|'We started the investigations without the dossier. We were proceeding with the investigations before we ever received that information,' McCabe told CNN. 'Was the dossier material important to the [FISA] package? Of course, it was. As was every fact included in that package. Was it the majority of what was in the package? Absolutely not.'}}
According to [[Ken Dilanian]], "The so-called dossier formed only a smart part of the evidence used to meet the legal burden of establishing 'probable cause' that Page was an agent of Russia."<ref name="Dilanian_7/23/2018">{{cite news |last=Dilanian |first=Ken |author-link=Ken Dilanian |date=July 23, 2018 |title=Why Team Trump is wrong about Carter Page, the dossier and that secret warrant |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/news/crime-courts/why-team-trump-wrong-about-carter-page-dossier-secret-warrant-n893666 |work=[[NBC News]] |access-date=June 19, 2023}}</ref>
The IG Report defended the FBI's use of Steele's reporting:<ref name="OIG_12/9/2019" />{{rp|vi}} {{blockquote| The fact that the FBI believed Steele had been retained to conduct political opposition research did not require the FBI, under either DOJ or FBI policy, to ignore his reporting. The FBI regularly receives information from individuals with potentially significant biases and motivations, including drug traffickers, convicted felons, and even terrorists. The FBI is not required to set aside such information; rather, FBI policy requires that it critically assess the information. We found that after receiving Steele's reporting, the Crossfire Hurricane team began those efforts in earnest.}}
=== Other conspiracy theories and false claims ===
{{see also|Russian interference in the 2016 United States elections#Trump's "Russiagate hoax" claims|Russia investigation origins conspiracy theory}} The founders of Fusion GPS did not expect their connections to [[Bruce Ohr|Bruce and Nellie Ohr]], Steele, Hillary Clinton, and the FBI to "become public and subsequently provide the framework for a deep-state conspiracy theory".<ref name="Walsh_11/22/2019" />
The dossier also figures as part of the [[Spygate (conspiracy theory)|Spygate conspiracy theory]] and [[conspiracy theories related to the Trump–Ukraine scandal]]. According to ''The Wall Street Journal'', President Trump's actions in the [[Trump–Ukraine scandal]] stemmed from his belief that Ukraine was responsible for the Steele Dossier.<ref name="Weber_10/17/2019">{{cite news |last=Weber |first=Peter |title=Rick Perry confirms Trump's Ukraine policy passed through Giuliani, recounts a wild call with Rudy |work=[[The Week]] |date=October 17, 2019 |url=https://theweek.com/speedreads/872395/rick-perry-confirms-trumps-ukraine-policy-passed-through-giuliani-recounts-wild-call-rudy |access-date=October 18, 2019}}</ref><ref name="Puko_Ballhaus_10/16/2019">{{cite news |last1=Puko |first1=Timothy |last2=Ballhaus |first2=Rebecca |author-link2=Rebecca Ballhaus |title=Rick Perry Called Rudy Giuliani at Trump's Direction on Ukraine Concerns |date=October 16, 2019 |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/rick-perry-called-rudy-giuliani-at-trumps-direction-on-ukraine-concerns-11571273635 |newspaper=[[The Wall Street Journal]] |access-date=October 24, 2019}}</ref> Trump has insinuated that the dossier had its origins in Ukraine, that the Clintons were involved, that Hillary Clinton's email server is currently secreted in Ukraine,<ref name="Bump_10/22/2019">{{cite news |last=Bump |first=Philip |title=It's probably worth remembering that Trump's Ukraine-2016 allegations are nonsensical |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=October 22, 2019 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/10/22/its-probably-worth-remembering-that-trumps-ukraine-allegations-are-nonsensical/ |access-date=October 24, 2019}}</ref> and that Clinton's deleted emails are in Ukraine.<ref name="Timm_9/25/2019" />
The dossier is central to Republican assertions that Trump is the victim of an intelligence community conspiracy to take him down. Democrats see this focus on the dossier as conspiratorial.<ref name="Swan_Brodey_10/18/2019">{{cite web |last1=Swan |first1=Betsy |last2=Brodey |first2=Sam |title=Nunes Tries to Use Steele Dossier to Defend Trump During Closed-Door Hearing | website=[[The Daily Beast]] |date=October 18, 2019 |url=https://www.thedailybeast.com/devin-nunes-raised-steele-dossier-in-closed-door-trump-impeachment-hearing-with-gordon-sondland |access-date=December 5, 2019}}</ref> Congressman [[Devin Nunes]], a staunch Trump defender, asserted as fact that the dossier originated in Ukraine during his questioning of EU ambassador [[Gordon Sondland]] during the [[Impeachment inquiry against Donald Trump|Trump impeachment inquiry]] hearings in September 2019.<ref name="Lambe_11/5/2019">{{cite web |last=Lambe |first=Jerry |title=Impeachment Inquiry Transcript Shows Devin Nunes Desperately Trying to Tie Steele Dossier to Ukraine | website=[[Law & Crime]] |date=November 5, 2019 |url=https://lawandcrime.com/high-profile/impeachment-inquiry-transcript-shows-devin-nunes-desperately-trying-to-tie-steele-dossier-to-ukraine/ |access-date=November 6, 2019}}</ref> Nunes also accused Hillary Clinton of colluding with Russia to get dirt on Trump. A fact-check by ''The Washington Post'' analyzed the claim and the role of the dossier. It found the claim to be false and gave it four Pinocchios.<ref name="Kessler_2/9/2018">{{cite news |last=Kessler |first=Glenn |author-link=Glenn Kessler (journalist) |date=February 9, 2018 |title=Did Hillary Clinton collude with the Russians to get 'dirt' on Trump to feed it to the FBI? |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/fact-checker/wp/2018/02/09/did-hillary-clinton-collude-with-the-russians-to-get-dirt-on-trump-to-feed-it-to-the-fbi/ |access-date=November 11, 2021 |quote=Finally, there is no evidence that Clinton was involved in Steele's reports or worked with Russian entities to feed information to Steele. That's where Nunes's claim goes off the rails – and why he earns Four Pinocchios.}}</ref>
According to Eric Lutz, [[Matt Gaetz]] and Jim Jordan have pushed a conspiracy theory that the dossier was "based on Russian disinformation".<ref name="Lutz_7/24/2019">{{cite web |last=Lutz |first=Eric |title=Republicans Use Mueller's Silence to Push Conspiracy Theories | website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]] |date=July 24, 2019 |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/news/2019/07/republicans-use-mueller-testimony-to-push-conspiracy-theories-jim-jordan-matt-gaetz |access-date=November 11, 2019 | quote=Gaetz used his allotted time to fire off a number of conspiracy theories, including that the dossier prepared by Christopher Steele was based on Russian disinformation.}}</ref> The inspector general's report concluded "that more should have been done to examine Steele's contacts with intermediaries of Russian oligarchs in order to assess those contacts as potential sources of disinformation that could have influenced Steele's reporting".<ref name="OIG_12/9/2019" />{{rp|386}}
[[David Frum]] also described a "suddenly red-hot media campaign to endorse Trump's fantasy that he was the victim of a 'Russia hoax.'" Frum argued that "anti-anti-Trump journalists want to use the Steele controversy to score points off politicians and media institutions that they dislike," and thus they "help him execute one of his Big Lies".<ref name="Frum_11/25/2021">{{Cite magazine |last=Frum |first=David |author-link=David Frum |date=November 25, 2021 |title=It Wasn't a Hoax |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/archive/2021/11/trump-russia-senate-intelligence-report/620815/ |access-date=December 11, 2021 |magazine=[[The Atlantic]]}}</ref>
== Denials of specific accusations == === Michael Cohen ===
[[File:Full Lanny Davis Interview- Michael Cohen Was 'Never, Ever' In Prague - MTP Daily - MSNBC.webm|thumb|Cohen's attorney, [[Lanny Davis]], says (at 8:50) Cohen was "never, ever" in Prague.]] Dossier source(s) allege that Cohen and three colleagues met Kremlin officials in the Prague offices of [[Rossotrudnichestvo]] in August 2016,<ref name="Stone_Gordon_4/13/2018" /><ref name="Withnall_Sengupta_1/12/2017" /><ref name="Cormier_5/5/2017" /> to arrange for payments to the hackers, cover up the hack,<ref name="Harding_5/10/2017" /><ref name="Borger_4/28/2017" /> and "cover up ties between Trump and Russia, including Manafort's involvement in Ukraine".<ref name="Sumter_11/16/2017" />
In 2016, Cohen told ''Mother Jones'' that he had visited Prague briefly 14 years before.<ref name="Corn_4/20/2018">{{cite magazine |last=Corn |first=David |author-link=David Corn |date=April 20, 2018 |title=Michael Cohen Says He's "Never" Been to Prague. He Told Me a Different Story. |magazine=[[Mother Jones (magazine)|Mother Jones]] |url=https://www.motherjones.com/politics/2018/04/michael-cohen-says-hes-never-been-to-prague-he-told-me-a-different-story/ |access-date=April 20, 2018}}</ref> ''[[The Wall Street Journal]]'' reported Cohen telling them (at an unspecified date) that he had been in Prague in 2001.<ref name="Berzon_Maremont_Rothfeld_1/11/2017">{{cite news |last1=Berzon |first1=Alexandra |author-link1=Alexandra Berzon |last2=Maremont |first2=Mark |author-link2=Mark Maremont |last3=Rothfeld |first3=Michael |author-link3=Michael Rothfeld |title=Intelligence Dossier Puts Longtime Trump Fixer in Spotlight |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/intelligence-dossier-puts-longtime-trump-fixer-in-spotlight-1484178320 |access-date=September 4, 2021 |newspaper=[[The Wall Street Journal]] |date=January 11, 2017}}</ref> Following the dossier's publication, as well as after subsequent reports, Cohen repeatedly denied having ever been to Prague.<ref name="Cormier_5/5/2017">{{cite news |last=Cormier |first=Anthony |title=This Is The Inside Of Trump's Lawyer's Passport |work=[[BuzzFeed News]] |date=May 5, 2017 |url=https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/anthonycormier/trumps-lawyer-showed-you-the-cover-of-his-passport-heres |access-date=December 24, 2017}}</ref><ref name="Berzon_Maremont_Rothfeld_1/11/2017" /><ref name="Boboltz_12/28/2018">{{cite web |last=Boboltz |first=Sara |title=Michael Cohen Denies Report Tying Him To Infamous Prague Trip | website=[[HuffPost]] |date=December 28, 2018 |url=https://www.huffingtonpost.com/entry/michael-cohen-prague-cell-phone_us_5c25244fe4b05c88b6fe7dd9 |access-date=December 29, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Bump_4/14/2018">{{cite news |last=Bump |first=Philip |title=Michael Cohen's visiting Prague would be a huge development in the Russia investigation |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=April 14, 2018 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/politics/wp/2018/04/14/michael-cohen-visiting-prague-would-be-a-huge-development-in-the-russia-investigation/ |access-date=July 4, 2018}}</ref> Cohen's attorney [[Lanny Davis]] said Cohen was "never, ever" in Prague, and that all allegations mentioning Cohen in the Steele dossier were false.<ref name="MSNBC_Davis_8/22/2018">{{cite news |url=https://www.msnbc.com/mtp-daily/watch/full-lanny-davis-interview-cohen-was-never-ever-in-prague-1304432195563?v=raila |title=Full Lanny Davis Interview: Cohen was 'never, ever' in Prague |subject=[[Lanny Davis]] |interviewer=[[Chuck Todd]] |work=[[MSNBC]] |minutes=9 |date=August 22, 2018 |access-date=August 25, 2018}}</ref>
Cohen's passport showed that he entered Italy in early July 2016, and that he left Italy in mid-July 2016, from [[Rome, Italy]].<ref name="Cormier_5/5/2017" /> ''Buzzfeed News'' in May 2017 reported Cohen telling them that he was in [[Capri, Italy]], for the time period in question with his family, friends, and musician and actor [[Steven van Zandt]], and that receipts would prove he had been on Capri, but he declined to provide them.<ref name="Cormier_5/5/2017" /> [[Roger Friedman]] reported sources quoting van Zandt's wife, Maureen, as saying that she and Steven were in Rome, and never traveled to Capri.<ref name="LeTourneau_12/18/2018">{{cite magazine |last=LeTourneau |first=Nancy |title=Cohen may have lost his alibi on Prague trip |magazine=[[Washington Monthly]] |date=December 18, 2018 |url=https://washingtonmonthly.com/2018/12/18/cohen-may-have-lost-his-alibi-on-prague-trip/ |access-date=December 20, 2018}}</ref> From this, Nancy LeTourneau of ''[[Washington Monthly]]'' commented that Cohen may have "lost his alibi", and thus doubted if he had really not traveled to Prague.<ref name="LeTourneau_12/18/2018" /> A December 2018 statement from Steven van Zandt's Twitter said that he had met Cohen in Rome and not traveled together.<ref name="Friedman_12/27/2018">{{cite web |last=Friedman |first=Roger |author-link=Roger Friedman |title=Rocker Steve van Zandt Clears Up Trump Lawyer Michael Cohen's Claim of Hanging Out in Capri, Summer 2016 | website=[[Roger Friedman|Showbiz411]] |date=December 27, 2018 |url=https://www.showbiz411.com/2018/12/27/rocker-steve-van-zandt-clears-up-trump-lawyer-michael-cohens-claim-of-hanging-out-in-capri-summer-2016 |access-date=September 16, 2021 |quote=Haha not exactly. Ran into him in the courtyard of our hotel in Rome. He wanted me to meet his wife. Which I did. Very nice. That was it. We've never gone anywhere together.}}</ref> In March 2019, ''The Washington Post'' reported that Cohen had been in Capri and Rome during his Italy trip, and that Cohen said he met Steven van Zandt in Rome.<ref name="Helderman_Roig-Franzia_3/1/2019">{{cite news |last1=Helderman |first1=Rosalind |author-link1=Rosalind S. Helderman |last2=Roig-Franzia |first2=Manuel |title=Two days in July: As Republicans convened in Cleveland, did Trump receive a heads-up about WikiLeaks? |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/two-days-in-july-as-republicans-convened-in-cleveland-did-trump-receive-a-heads-up-about-wikileaks/2019/03/01/064d3ab6-3b6d-11e9-aaae-69364b2ed137_story.html |access-date=September 4, 2021 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=March 1, 2019}}</ref>
Cohen has also stated that he was in Los Angeles between August 23 and 29, and in New York for the entire month of September.<ref name="Gray_1/10/2017">{{cite magazine |last=Gray |first=Rosie |author-link=Rosie Gray |title=Michael Cohen: 'It Is Fake News Meant to Malign Mr. Trump' |magazine=[[The Atlantic]] |date=January 10, 2017 |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2017/01/michael-cohen-it-is-fake-news-meant-to-malign-mr-trump/512762/ |access-date=December 24, 2017 |quote=I'm telling you emphatically that I've not been to Prague, I've never been to Czech [Republic], I've not been to Russia.}}</ref>
=== Aleksej Gubarev ===
Gubarev has denied all accusations made in the dossier.<ref name="Goldstein_Hall_Gordon_2/3/2017" /><ref name="Hall_9/21/2018" /> The accusations are twofold, as they mention Gubarev and his companies. While there is evidence that Gubarev's companies may have been used to facilitate cybercrimes,<ref name="Rosenberg_3/14/2019" /><ref name="Hall_3/14/2019" /><ref name="Helderman_Hamburger_Nakashima_3/15/2019" /> Andrew Weisburd has said that "Neither ''BuzzFeed'' nor Steele have accused Gubarev of being a willing participant in wrongdoing."<ref name="Hall_9/21/2018" />
=== Paul Manafort ===
Manafort has "denied taking part in any collusion with the Russian state, but [[Foreign Agents Registration Act|registered himself as a foreign agent]] retroactively after it was revealed his firm received more than $17m working as a lobbyist for a pro-Russian Ukrainian party."<ref name="Borger_10/7/2017" />
=== Carter Page ===
Page originally denied meeting any Russian officials, but his later testimony, acknowledging that he had met with senior Russian officials at Rosneft, has been interpreted as corroboration of portions of the dossier.<ref name="Raju_Herb_Polantz_11/8/2017" /><ref name="Lanktree_11/7/2017">{{cite news |date=November 7, 2017 |first=Graham |last=Lanktree |title=Carter Page Attacked Christopher Steele's Trump Dossier But His Testimony Raised Questions Over Russian Meetings |work=[[Newsweek]] |url=https://www.newsweek.com/carter-page-testimony-attacks-christopher-steeles-trump-dossier-703691 |access-date=April 2, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Kelly_11/6/2017">{{cite news |date=November 6, 2017 |first=Erin |last=Kelly |title=Trump campaign adviser Carter Page acknowledges meeting with senior Russian officials: transcript |newspaper=[[USA Today]] |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2017/11/06/trump-campaign-adviser-carter-page-acknowledges-meeting-senior-russian-officials-transcript/838647001/ |access-date=April 2, 2018}}</ref> On February 11, 2021, Page lost a defamation suit he had filed against [[Yahoo! News]] and ''[[HuffPost]]'' for their articles describing his activities mentioned in the Steele dossier. The judge said: "The article simply says that U.S. intelligence agencies were investigating reports of plaintiff's meetings with Russian officials, which Plaintiff admits is true."<ref name="multiple" />
=== Donald Trump ===
{{see also|#Kompromat and "golden showers" allegations}} Trump addressed the "[[Golden shower (urolagnia)|golden showers]]" allegation in January 2017, publicly stating: "Does anyone really believe that story? I'm also very much of a [[germaphobe]], by the way."<ref name="Oliver_1/11/2017">{{cite news |last1=Oliver |first1=David |title=Trump on Allegations: I'm a 'Germaphobe' |url=https://www.usnews.com/news/national-news/articles/2017-01-11/trump-on-russia-allegations-im-a-germaphobe |access-date=May 13, 2021 |work=[[U.S. News & World Report]] |date=January 11, 2017}}</ref> This contradicted an interview in 2015 when he stated: "'I'm not germaphobic,' he said, when asked if he would kiss babies and shake hands on the campaign trail."<ref name="Oliver_1/11/2017" />
According to FBI Director James Comey, in several private conversations between him and Trump in early 2017, Trump denied the "golden showers" allegation and said he assumed he was always being recorded when in Russia.<ref name="Chait_4/13/2018" /> He also lied by repeatedly claiming that he did not stay overnight in Moscow even once<ref name="Foran_4/26/2018" /><ref name="Levin_5/23/2018">{{cite web | last=Levin | first=Bess | title=Trump's Pee-Tape Alibi Is Falling Apart | website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]] | date=April 23, 2018 | url=https://www.vanityfair.com/news/2018/04/trumps-pee-tape-alibi-is-falling-apart | access-date=April 18, 2024}}</ref><ref name="May_4/24/2018">{{cite web | last=May | first=Charlie | title=Donald Trump's Moscow trip alibi has a big hole | website=[[Salon.com|Salon]] | date=April 24, 2018 | url=https://www.salon.com/2018/04/24/donald-trumps-moscow-trip-alibi-has-a-big-hole/ | access-date=April 18, 2024}}</ref><ref name="Schreckinger_4/24/2018">{{cite web | last=Schreckinger | first=Ben | author-link=Ben Schreckinger | title=Trump's false claims to Comey about Moscow stay could aid Robert Mueller | website=[[Politico]] | date=April 24, 2018 | url=https://www.politico.eu/article/donald-trumps-false-claims-to-james-comey-about-moscow-stay-could-aid-robert-mueller/ | access-date=April 18, 2024}}</ref> at the time of the Miss Universe contest in 2013.<ref name="Chait_4/13/2018">{{cite magazine |last=Chait |first=Jonathan |author-link=Jonathan Chait |title=I'm a Peeliever and You Should Be, Too. 5 Reasons the Pee Tape Is Probably Real. |magazine=[[New York (magazine)|New York]] |date=April 13, 2018 |url=https://nymag.com/daily/intelligencer/2018/04/im-a-peeliever-and-you-should-be-too.html |access-date=April 16, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Silver_4/23/2018" /><ref name="Francis_Groll_6/7/2017">{{cite magazine |last1=Francis |first1=David |last2=Groll |first2=Elias |title=Comey: Trump Denied He Was Involved With 'Hookers' in Russia |magazine=[[Foreign Policy]] |date=January 25, 2017 |url=https://foreignpolicy.com/2017/06/07/comey-trump-denied-he-was-involved-with-hookers-in-russia/ |access-date=January 25, 2018}}</ref> That claim was contradicted by several types of sources, including flight records that document he stayed overnight in Moscow the full night before the pageant, then, during the night of the pageant, he left Moscow at 3:58 a.m.<ref name="Kirell_4/24/2018" /><ref name="Helderman_9/22/2018">{{cite news |last=Helderman |first=Rosalind S. |author-link=Rosalind S. Helderman |title=How a British music publicist ended up in the middle of the Russia storm |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=September 22, 2018 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/how-a-british-music-publicist-ended-up-in-the-middle-of-the-russia-storm/2018/09/21/d1449a40-ba83-11e8-a8aa-860695e7f3fc_story.html |access-date=September 23, 2018}}</ref>
According to Comey, Trump's false denials about whether he had stayed overnight in Moscow, despite Comey not asking about it, exhibited behavior that "tends to reflect [[consciousness of guilt]]", but this conclusion is "not definitive", said Comey.<ref>Multiple sources: * {{cite news |title=Comey: Trump's Russia trip claim reflects guilt |work=[[CNN]] |date=April 26, 2018 |url=https://www.cnn.com/videos/politics/2018/04/25/james-comey-town-hall-consciousness-of-guilt-trump-russia-trip.cnn |access-date=May 9, 2021 |ref=none}} * {{cite news |last=Bump |first=Philip |title=Analysis: When you are and aren't legally allowed to lie to the FBI |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=April 26, 2018 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/politics/wp/2018/04/26/when-you-are-and-arent-legally-allowed-to-lie-to-the-fbi/ |access-date=May 9, 2021 |ref=none}} * {{cite web |last=Schreckinger |first=Ben |author-link=Ben Schreckinger |title=Trump's false claims to Comey about Moscow stay could aid Mueller |newspaper=[[Politico]] |date=April 23, 2018 |url=https://www.politico.com/story/2018/04/23/trump-moscow-overnight-stay-mueller-comey-545834 |access-date=November 14, 2019 |ref=none}}</ref><ref name="Lemire_Day_4/27/2018" />
Trump publicly disputed that he had issued such a denial to Comey: "He said I didn't stay there a night. Of course I stayed there. ... I stayed there a very short period of time but of course I stayed."<ref name="Lemire_Day_4/27/2018">{{cite news |last1=Lemire |first1=Jonathan |last2=Day |first2=Chad |title=Trump pledges hands off Russia probe, may "change my mind' |agency=[[Associated Press]] |date=April 27, 2018 |url=https://apnews.com/article/north-america-donald-trump-ap-top-news-elections-james-comey-27a9d905748d4141b0e675afc6356527 |access-date=May 10, 2021}}</ref>
== Reactions to dossier ==
[[File:House-Intel-Glenn-Simpson-Transcript.pdf|thumb|November 14, 2017{{snd}}[[House Intelligence Committee]] transcript of [[Glenn R. Simpson|Glenn Simpson]]]] {{Wikisource|House Intelligence Committee Interview of Glenn Simpson|House Intelligence Committee Interview of Glenn Simpson Transcript}} [[File:Simpson-transcript-redacted.pdf|thumb|August 22, 2017, Fusion GPS testimony transcript of [[Glenn R. Simpson|Glenn Simpson]]]] {{Wikisource|Senate Judiciary Committee Interview of Glenn Simpson}}
=== Descriptions ===
The dossier has been described with many negative epithets:
Trump has labeled the dossier "fake news", a "political witch hunt",<ref name="Ewing_1/10/2017" /> "debunked", "fictitious", and "discredited".<ref name="Sengupta_4/20/2018" /><ref name="Breuninger_1/13/2018" /><ref name="Stefansky_11/11/2017" /> Many mainstream sources have also described the dossier as "discredited".{{efn|name="discredited"|"discredited"<ref name="Roig-Franzia_10/7/2024" /><ref name="CBS 03/07/2025">{{cite web|url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-targets-law-firm-perkins-coie-2016-russia-investigation/|title=Trump signs executive order yanking security clearances of lawyers from Perkins Coie|publisher=CBS News|date=March 7, 2025|accessdate=March 8, 2025}}</ref><ref name="Savage_Goldman_Feuer_1/23/2025">{{cite web | last1=Savage | first1=Charlie | last2=Goldman | first2=Adam | last3=Feuer | first3=Alan | title=Kash Patel, Trump's F.B.I. Pick, Pushed False Claims About Trump Investigations | website=[[The New York Times]] | date=January 23, 2025 | url=https://www.nytimes.com/2025/01/23/us/politics/kash-patel-trump-fbi-inquiries.html | access-date=February 22, 2025}}</ref><ref name="Sakellariadis_11/12/2024">{{cite web | last=Sakellariadis | first=John | title=Trump picks close ally John Ratcliffe for CIA director | website=[[Politico]] | date=November 12, 2024 | url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/11/12/john-ratcliffe-cia-trump-00189143 | access-date=February 22, 2025}}</ref><ref name="McMaster_8/23/2024">{{cite web | last=McMaster | first=H. R. | title='I Cannot Understand Putin's Hold on Trump' | website=[[The Wall Street Journal]] | date=August 23, 2024 | url=https://www.wsj.com/politics/i-cannot-understand-putins-hold-on-trump-8d045461 | language=en-US | access-date=February 22, 2025}}</ref><ref name="Slisco_6/2/2024">{{cite web | last=Slisco | first=Aila | title=Elon Musk's "Steele Dossier" post gets brutal fact checks | website=[[Newsweek]] | date=June 2, 2024 | url=https://www.newsweek.com/elon-musks-steele-dossier-post-gets-brutal-fact-checks-1907228 | access-date=February 22, 2025}}</ref>}}
On January 4, 2018, U.S. District Court Judge [[Amit Mehta|Amit P. Mehta]] ruled on Trump's repeated tweets describing the dossier as "fake" or "discredited":<ref name="Gerstein_1/4/2018">{{cite web |last=Gerstein |first=Josh |title=Judge: Trump tweets don't require more disclosure on dossier | website=[[Politico]] |date=January 4, 2018 |url=https://www.politico.com/blogs/under-the-radar/2018/01/04/trump-dossier-tweets-judge-ruling-326137 |access-date=August 18, 2018}}</ref> {{blockquote| None of the tweets inescapably lead to the inference that the President's statements about the Dossier are rooted in information he received from the law enforcement and intelligence communities. ... The President's statements may very well be based on media reports or his own personal knowledge, or could simply be viewed as political statements intended to counter media accounts about the Russia investigation, rather than assertions of pure fact.}}
[[Adam Goldman]] and [[Charlie Savage (author)|Charlie Savage]] called it "deeply flawed".<ref name="Goldman_Savage_7/25/2020" /> Savage described it as generally unreliable.<ref name="Savage_12/1/2021">{{cite news |last=Savage |first=Charlie |author-link=Charlie Savage (author) |date=April 5, 2022 |orig-date=updated after initial publication on December 1, 2021 |title=Why the Discredited Dossier Does Not Undercut the Russia Investigation |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/12/01/us/trump-russia-investigation-dossier.html |access-date=August 22, 2022 | quote=Was the dossier a reliable source of information? No. It has become clear over time that its sourcing was thin and sketchy. No corroborating evidence has emerged in intervening years to support many of the specific claims in the dossier, and government investigators determined that one key allegation – that Mr. Trump's lawyer, Michael Cohen, had met with Russian officials in Prague during the campaign – was false. When the F.B.I. interviewed Mr. Danchenko in 2017, he told the bureau that he thought the tenor of the dossier was more conclusive than was justified; for example, Mr. Danchenko portrayed the blackmail tape story as rumors and speculation that he was not able to confirm. He also said a key source had called him without identifying himself, and that he had guessed at the source's identity.}}</ref> Both have also described it as a "compendium of rumors and unproven assertions".<ref name="Goldman_Savage_11/4/2021" /> Ryan Lucas described it as an "explosive dossier of unsubstantiated and salacious material about President Trump's alleged ties with Russia".<ref name="Lucas_8/23/2017" />
=== Trump ===
Donald Trump's first Twitter reaction to the dossier was a January 10, 2017, tweet: "FAKE NEWS—A TOTAL POLITICAL WITCH HUNT!".<ref name="Ewing_1/10/2017" /> Trump has repeatedly denied collusion with Russia<ref name="Lee_12/26/2017" /> and criticized the intelligence and media sources that published the dossier.<ref name="Henderson_Lawler_Burke_1/12/2017">{{cite news |last1=Henderson |first1=Barney |last2=Lawler |first2=David |last3=Burke |first3=Louise |title=Donald Trump attacks alleged Russian dossier as 'fake news' and slams Buzzfeed and CNN at press conference |newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph]] |date=January 12, 2017 |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2017/01/11/donald-trump-kremlin-blast-fabricated-report-russian-ties-asfbi/ |access-date=February 19, 2018}}</ref> During a press conference on January 11, 2017, Trump denounced the dossier's claims as false, saying it was "disgraceful" for U.S. intelligence agencies to report them.<ref name="Rascoe_1/11/2017">{{cite news |last=Rascoe |first=Ayesha |author-link=Ayesha Rascoe |title=Trump accuses U.S. spy agencies of Nazi practices over 'phony' Russia dossier |work=[[Reuters]] |date=January 11, 2017 |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-usa-trump/russia-likely-main-topic-in-trumps-first-news-conference-since-election-idUSKBN14V18L |access-date=February 19, 2018}}</ref> In response to Trump's criticism, CNN said it had published "carefully sourced reporting" on the matter that had been "matched by the other major news organizations".<ref name="Sutton_1/11/2017">{{cite web |last1=Sutton |first1=Kelsey |date=January 11, 2017 |title=Trump calls CNN 'fake news', as channel defends its reporting on intelligence briefing |url=https://www.politico.com/blogs/on-media/2017/01/trump-refusing-to-answer-question-from-cnn-reporter-you-are-fake-news-233485 | website=[[Politico]] |access-date=January 11, 2017}}</ref>
A footnote in the Mueller Report revealed that Trump requested James Comey and James Clapper to publicly refute the dossier. The two men then exchanged emails about the request, with Clapper saying that "Trump wanted him to say the dossier was 'bogus, which, of course, I can't do'."<ref name="Pecorin_5/1/2019">{{cite news |last=Pecorin |first=Allison |title=10 best footnotes of the Mueller report |work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]] |date=May 1, 2019 |url=https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/10-best-footnotes-mueller-report/story?id=62529749 |access-date=November 6, 2019}}</ref><ref name="Nicholas_11/21/2019" />
On July 11, 2020, Trump tweeted that Steele should be [[extradition|extradited]].<ref name="O'Connell_7/11/2020">{{cite news |last=O'Connell |first=Oliver |title=Trump demands extradition of former MI6 officer Christopher Steele over Russia dossier |newspaper=[[The Independent]] |date=July 11, 2020 |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-twitter-russia-report-christopher-steele-mi6-a9613711.html |access-date=July 31, 2020}}</ref>
=== Mueller Report ===
In July 2019, Special Agent in Charge David Archey briefed the [[United States Senate Select Committee on Intelligence|Senate Intelligence Committee]] about certain aspects of the [[Mueller special counsel investigation|Special Counsel Office's]] (SCO) "investigative process and information management":<ref name="SICv5_8/18/2020" />{{rp|852}} {{blockquote| We [the SCO] were aware of the Steele dossier, obviously. We were aware of some of the efforts that went into its verification. ... we did not include Steele dossier reporting in the report. ... [T]hose allegations go to the heart of things that were in our mandate—but we believed our own investigation. The information that we collected would have superseded it, and been something we would have relied on more, and that's why you see what we did in the report and not the Steele dossier in the report.}}
"Archey declined to provide further information on whether FBI or SCO attempted to verify information in the dossier, although he noted that the SCO did not draw on the dossier to support its conclusions."<ref name="SICv5_8/18/2020" />{{rp|852}}
=== Others ===
Russia calls it an "absolute fabrication" and "a hoax intended to further damage {{Nowrap|U.S.–Russian}} relations".<ref name="Ewing_1/10/2017">{{cite news |last=Ewing |first=Philip |title=Trump Denies Allegations Of Secret Ties, Collusion Between Campaign And Russia |work=[[NPR]] |date=January 10, 2017 |url=https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2017/01/10/509223836/trump-denies-allegations-of-secret-ties-collusion-between-campaign-and-russia |access-date=November 22, 2019}}</ref>
As Putin's press secretary, Peskov insisted in an interview that "I can assure you that the allegations in this funny paper, in this so-called report, they are untrue. They are all fake."<ref name="Neely_1/21/2017">{{cite news |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/putin-spokesman-u-s-intelligence-report-russian-hacking-ridiculous-n706096 |title=Kremlin Spokesman: U.S. Intelligence Report on Russian Hacking 'Ridiculous' |last=Neely |first=Bill |author-link=Bill Neely |date=January 21, 2017 |work=[[NBC News]] |access-date=January 15, 2017}}</ref> Putin called the people who leaked the dossier "worse than prostitutes"<ref name="Walker_1/17/2017">{{cite news |last=Walker |first=Shaun |title=Putin says those behind Trump dossier are 'worse than prostitutes' |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=January 17, 2017 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2017/jan/17/trump-dossier-a-crude-provocation-compiled-by-a-fugitive-crook-says-russian-minister |access-date=February 21, 2020}}</ref> and referred to the dossier itself as "rubbish".<ref name="Dewan_Veselinovic_1/17/2017">{{cite news |url=https://www.cnn.com/2017/01/17/politics/russia-us-putin-trump/index.html |title=Putin on Trump dossier claims: 'Rubbish' |first1=Angela |last1=Dewan |first2=Milena |last2=Veselinovic |work=[[CNN]] |date=January 17, 2017 |access-date=January 17, 2017}}</ref> Putin went on to state he believed the dossier was "clearly fake",<ref name="Macfarquhar_1/17/2017">{{cite news |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2017/01/17/world/europe/putin-trump-memo.html |title=Putin Says Accusations in Trump Dossier Are 'Clearly Fake' |last=Macfarquhar |first=Neil |author-link=Neil MacFarquhar |date=January 17, 2017 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |access-date=January 18, 2017}}</ref> fabricated as a plot against the legitimacy of President-elect Trump.<ref name="Roth_Filipov_1/17/2017">{{cite news |last1=Roth |first1=Andrew |last2=Filipov |first2=David |title=Russia's Putin rejects Trump dossier report as plot against 'legitimacy' of president-elect |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=January 17, 2017 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/waiting-for-trump-russia-hopes-for-closer-cooperation-on-syria-war-on-terror/2017/01/17/d24785b0-dc86-11e6-b2cf-b67fe3285cbc_story.html |access-date=February 19, 2018}}</ref>
Some of Steele's former colleagues expressed support for his character, saying "The idea his work is fake or a cowboy operation is false—completely untrue. Chris is an experienced and highly regarded professional. He's not the sort of person who will simply pass on gossip."<ref name="Harding_Hopkins_1/12/2017">{{cite news |last1=Harding |first1=Luke |author-link1=Luke Harding |last2=Hopkins |first2=Nick |author-link2=Nick Hopkins |title=Donald Trump dossier: intelligence sources vouch for author's credibility |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |date=January 12, 2017 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2017/jan/12/intelligence-sources-vouch-credibility-donald-trump-russia-dossier-author |access-date=January 23, 2018}}</ref>
Among journalists, [[Bob Woodward]] called the dossier a "garbage document".<ref name="Mahtani_1/17/2017">{{cite news |last=Mahtani |first=Melissa |title=Woodward: The CNN story was 'exactly right' |work=[[CNN]] |date=January 17, 2017 |url=https://www.cnn.com/2017/01/17/politics/woodward-bernstein-talk-trump-dossier-cnntv/index.html |access-date=February 21, 2020 | quote=Asked by CNN's Alisyn Camerota to explain his position, Woodward said, 'I have read those 35 pages, and the quality is not good.'}}</ref> [[Chuck Todd]] of CBS News labeled the report "fake news".<ref name="Shafer_12/22/2018" /> [[Carl Bernstein]] said "It's not fake news, otherwise senior-most intelligence chiefs would not have done this."<ref name="KABC_1/16/2017">{{cite web |date=January 16, 2017 |title=Bob Woodward: Intel Dossier on Trump is "Garbage" |publisher=[[KABC (AM)]] |url=https://www.kabc.com/2017/01/16/bob-woodward-intel-dossier-on-trump-is-garbage/ |access-date=February 20, 2020}}</ref> [[Ben Smith (journalist)|Ben Smith]], editor of ''BuzzFeed'', wrote: "The dossier is a document ... of obvious central public importance. It's the subject of multiple investigations by intelligence agencies, by Congress. That was clear a year ago. It's a lot clearer now."<ref name="Greenwood_1/10/2018">{{cite news |last=Greenwood |first=Max |title=BuzzFeed editor defends publication of dossier |newspaper=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]] |date=January 10, 2018 |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/media/368442-buzzfeed-editor-defends-publication-of-dossier |access-date=March 31, 2018}}</ref>
[[Ynet]], an Israeli online news site, reported on January 12, 2017, that U.S. intelligence advised Israeli intelligence officers to be cautious about sharing information with the incoming Trump administration, until the possibility of Russian influence over Trump, suggested by Steele's report, has been fully investigated.<ref name="Bergman_1/12/2017">{{cite news |first=Ronen |last=Bergman |url=https://www.ynetnews.com/articles/0,7340,L-4906642,00.html |title=US intel sources warn Israel against sharing secrets with Trump administration |work=[[Ynetnews]] |date=January 12, 2017 |access-date=April 1, 2018}}</ref>
On January 2, 2018, Simpson and Fritsch authored an op-ed in ''The New York Times'', requesting that Republicans "release full transcripts of our firm's testimony" and further wrote that, "the Steele dossier was not the trigger for the FBI's investigation into Russian meddling. As we told the Senate Judiciary Committee in August, our sources said the dossier was taken so seriously because it corroborated reports the bureau had received from other sources, including one inside the Trump camp."<ref name="Simpson_Fritsch_1/2/2018">{{cite news |last1=Simpson |first1=Glenn R. |last2=Fritsch |first2=Peter |title=The Republicans' Fake investigations |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=January 2, 2018 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2018/01/02/opinion/republicans-investigation-fusion-gps.html |access-date=January 3, 2018}}</ref> Ken Dilanian of NBC News said a "source close to Fusion GPS" told him the FBI had not planted anyone in the Trump camp, but rather that Simpson was referring to Papadopoulos.<ref name="Shafer_1/13/2018">{{cite web |last=Shafer |first=Jack |author-link=Jack Shafer |title=Week 34: The Dossier Strikes Back |magazine=[[Politico]] |date=January 13, 2018 |url=https://www.politico.com/magazine/story/2018/01/13/jack-shafer-swamp-diary-trump-dossier-216313/ |access-date=January 21, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Jalonick_1/9/2018" />
On January 5, 2018, in the first known Congressional [[criminal referral]] resulting from investigations related to the Russian interference in the 2016 U.S. election, Grassley made a referral to the Justice Department suggesting that they investigate possible criminal charges against Steele<ref name="Stanglin_1/5/2018">{{cite news |last=Stanglin |first=Doug |title=GOP senators recommend criminal probe of 'Steele dossier' author |newspaper=[[USA Today]] |date=January 5, 2018 |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/2018/01/05/gop-senators-recommend-criminal-probe-steele-dossier-author/1008199001/ |access-date=January 7, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Fandos_Rosenberg_12/15/2017">{{cite news |last1=Fandos |first1=Nicholas |author-link1=Nicholas Fandos |last2=Rosenberg |first2=Matthew |author-link2=Matthew Rosenberg |title=Republican Senators Raise Possible Charges Against Author of Trump Dossier |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=December 15, 2017 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2018/01/05/us/politics/christopher-steele-dossier-judiciary-committee.html |access-date=January 6, 2018}}</ref> for allegedly making false statements to the FBI about the distribution of the dossier's claims,<ref name="Sheth_2/7/2018">{{cite web |last=Sheth |first=Sonam |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/declassified-grassley-graham-letter-steele-dossier-nunes-memo-2018-2 |title=Congress just declassified a letter that offers critical clues about the Steele dossier and the Nunes memo | website=[[Business Insider]] |date=February 7, 2018 |access-date=February 8, 2018}}</ref> specifically possible "inconsistencies" in what Steele told authorities and "possibly lying to FBI officials".<ref name="Price_1/5/2018">{{cite news |last=Price |first=Greg |date=January 5, 2018 |title=Trump–Russia Dossier Author Should Face Criminal Investigation for Lying to FBI, Top Republicans Recommend |work=[[Newsweek]] |url=https://www.newsweek.com/trump-russia-dossier-steele-investigation-772587 |access-date=February 19, 2018}}</ref> Senator [[Lindsey Graham]] also signed the letter.<ref name="Barrett_Hamburger_1/5/2018">{{cite news |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/senior-republican-refers-trump-dossier-author-for-possible-charges/2018/01/05/ead4585e-f0af-11e7-b390-a36dc3fa2842_story.html |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=January 5, 2018 |access-date=January 5, 2018 |first1=Devlin |last1=Barrett |first2=Tom |last2=Hamburger |author-link2=Tom Hamburger |title=Senior Republican refers Trump dossier author for possible charges}}</ref><ref name="Tau_1/5/2018">{{cite news |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/senators-ask-justice-department-to-open-criminal-probe-into-trump-dossier-author-1515180982 |title=Senators Ask Justice Department to Open Criminal Probe Into Trump Dossier Author |last=Tau |first=Byron |author-link=Byron Tau |date=January 5, 2018 |newspaper=[[The Wall Street Journal]] |access-date=January 5, 2018}}</ref> Both Grassley and Graham declared that they were not alleging that Steele "had committed any crime. Rather, they had passed on the information for 'further investigation only'."<ref name="BBC_News_1/5/2018">{{cite news |title=Republicans urge investigation into Trump dossier author |work=[[BBC News]] |date=January 5, 2018 |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-42586855 |access-date=January 7, 2018}}</ref> The referral was met with skepticism from legal experts, as well as some of the other Republicans and Democrats on the Judiciary committee, who reportedly had not been consulted.<ref name="Barrett_Hamburger_1/5/2018" />
On January 8, 2018, a spokesman for Grassley said he did not plan to release the transcript of Simpson's August 22, 2017, testimony before the SJC.<ref name="Dennis_1/8/2018">{{cite news |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2018-01-08/grassley-won-t-release-fusion-gps-transcript-from-russia-probe |title=Grassley Won't Release Fusion GPS Transcript From Russia Probe |last=Dennis |first=Steven T. |date=January 8, 2017 |work=[[Bloomberg News]] |access-date=January 9, 2018}}</ref> The next day, ranking committee member Senator [[Dianne Feinstein]] unilaterally released the transcript.<ref name="Cheney_1/9/2018">{{cite web |last=Cheney |first=Kyle |date=January 9, 2018 |url=https://www.politico.com/story/2018/01/09/feinstein-releases-transcript-of-interview-with-fusion-gps-co-founder-329573 |title=Feinstein releases transcript of interview with Fusion GPS co-founder |newspaper=[[Politico]] |access-date=January 9, 2018}}</ref><ref name="Barrett_Hamburger_1/9/2018">{{cite news |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/national-security/feinstein-releases-testimony-of-glenn-simpson-whose-research-firm-fusion-gps-was-behind-trump-dossier/2018/01/09/15da150a-f562-11e7-beb6-c8d48830c54d_story.html |title=Fusion GPS founder told Senate investigators the FBI had a whistleblower in Trump's network |last1=Barrett |first1=Devlin |last2=Hamburger |first2=Tom |author-link2=Tom Hamburger |date=January 9, 2018 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |access-date=January 9, 2018}}</ref>
On January 10, 2018, Fox News host [[Sean Hannity]] appeared to have advance information on the forthcoming release of the Nunes memo and its assertions about the dossier, saying "more shocking information will be coming out in just days that will show systemic FISA abuse". Hannity asserted that this new information would reveal "a totally phony document full of Russian lies and propaganda that was then used by the Obama administration to surveil members of an opposition party and incoming president," adding that this was "the real Russia collusion story" that represented a "precipice of one of the largest abuses of power in U.S. American history. And I'm talking about the literal shredding of the U.S. Constitution."<ref name="Fernandez_2/5/2018">{{cite web |last=Fernandez |first=Nick |title=The Nunes memo perfectly lined up with what Hannity has been hyping for weeks prior to its release | website=[[Media Matters for America]] |date=February 5, 2018 |url=https://www.mediamatters.org/research/2018/02/05/nunes-memo-perfectly-lined-what-hannity-has-been-hyping-weeks-prior-its-release/219276 |access-date=April 25, 2018}}</ref>
In April 2018, the [[White House Correspondents' Association]] (WHCA) gave [[White House Correspondents' Association#WHCA Award for Excellence in Presidential News Coverage Under Deadline Pressure|The Merriman Smith Memorial Award]] to CNN reporters Evan Perez, [[Jim Sciutto]], [[Jake Tapper]] and [[Carl Bernstein]]. In January 2017, they reported that the intelligence community had briefed Obama and Trump of allegations that Russians claimed to have "compromising personal and financial information" on then-President elect Donald Trump.<ref name="Perez_Sciutto_Tapper_Bernstein_1/10/2017" /><ref name="Calderone_4/2/2018">{{cite web |last1=Calderone |first1=Michael |title=Trump's correspondents' dinner decision gets complicated |url=https://www.politico.com/story/2018/04/02/trump-journalists-correspondents-dinner-495858 |newspaper=[[Politico]] |date=April 2, 2018 |access-date=April 8, 2018}}</ref>
As late as July 29, 2018, Trump continued to falsely insist the FBI investigation of Russian interference was initiated because of the dossier, and three days later White House press secretary [[Sarah Sanders]] repeated the false assertion. Fox News host [[Shepard Smith]] said of Trump's assertion: "In the main and in its parts, that statement is patently false."<ref name="Dicker_8/1/2018">{{cite web |last=Dicker |first=Rachel |date=August 1, 2018 |title=Fox News' Shepard Smith Calls Out Trump, White House for Claim About Russia Probe: 'Patently False' | website=[[Mediaite]] |url=https://www.mediaite.com/tv/fox-news-shepard-smith-calls-out-trump-white-house-for-claim-about-russia-probe-patently-false/ |access-date=August 1, 2018}}</ref>
[[Alan Huffman]], an expert on opposition research, has compared the two forms of opposition research represented by the dossier and WikiLeaks. He didn't believe the dossier's intelligence gathering to be illegitimate, although "a little strange", while he was troubled by the large dump of documents from WikiLeaks that "may have been obtained in an illegal way".<ref name="Krieger_10/31/2017">{{cite web |last=Krieger |first=Hilary |title=An Introduction To The Dark Arts Of Opposition Research | website=[[FiveThirtyEight]] |date=October 31, 2017 |url=https://fivethirtyeight.com/features/an-introduction-to-the-dark-arts-of-opposition-research/ |access-date=April 27, 2019}}</ref>
In March 2020, Steele commented publicly on the dossier, stating: "I stand by the integrity of our work, our sources and what we did."<ref name="Allen_3/7/2020">{{cite news |last1=Allen |first1=Nick |title=Christopher Steele breaks silence over Trump-Russia dossier and says Mueller report was 'too narrow' |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2020/03/07/christopher-steele-breaks-silence-trump-russia-dossier-says/ |access-date=August 1, 2020 |newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph]] |date=March 7, 2020}}</ref>
=== Litigation ===
{{Main|Litigation involving Steele dossier}} Multiple lawsuits have been filed in connection with the Steele dossier, primarily involving [[defamation]] claims by plaintiffs such as [[Aleksei Gubarev|Aleksej Gubarev]], the three owners of [[Alfa-Bank]] ([[Mikhail Fridman]], [[Petr Aven]], and [[German Khan]]), Michael Cohen, Devin Nunes, Giorgi Rtskhiladze, Carter Page, and Trump. They have been filed against Christopher Steele, Orbis Business Intelligence, BuzzFeed, [[Yahoo! Inc. (2017–present)|Oath]], the Democratic National Committee, and others. All of these defamation cases, except one, were dismissed or withdrawn by the plaintiffs.
In ''[[Litigation involving Steele dossier#Trump v. Orbis Business Intelligence|Trump v. Orbis Business Intelligence]]'', Trump accepted "that the Defendant is not responsible in law for the publication of the Memoranda by BuzzFeed".<ref name="TrumpvOrbis_2/1/2024">{{cite web | date=February 1, 2024 | title=President Donald J. Trump -V- Orbis Business Intelligence Limited, Case No. KB-2022-004403 | website=[[Royal Courts of Justice]], Strand, London | url=https://www.judiciary.uk/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Trump-v-Orbis-Judgment.pdf | access-date=May 16, 2024}}</ref>
Carter Page unsuccessfully sought damages in a lawsuit against the [[United States Department of Justice]], the Federal Bureau of Investigation, and several former U.S. government officials, alleging violations of his constitutional rights. Trump also filed unsuccessful lawsuits against Hillary Clinton, Steele, Orbis, and others.
